title: potentilla reptans var. sessilis
intro: Potentilla reptans var. sessilis, also known as dwarf cinquefoil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been shown to have antioxidant, antimicrobial, and immunomodulatory effects, contributing to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Potentilla reptans var. sessilis can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
